organisations.name: Tynesoft
organisations.summary: Blaydon-associated company moving from educational software into games, publishing, ports, licences and support for multiple incompatible 8-bit computers.
organisations.role: Publishing, development, conversion, cassette and disk product pipeline.
organisations.unresolved: Exact legal/trading names, directors, premises and closure documentation still need official corroboration.

organisations.name: MicroValue
organisations.summary: Budget label associated with Tynesoft and the broad cassette catalogue.
organisations.unresolved: Ownership and trading status should be checked against packaging, adverts and company records.

organisations.name: Icon Software
organisations.summary: Fragmented public record connecting early BBC/Electron authors with Icon releases before some work moved through Audiogenic/ASL.
organisations.role: Early publisher/development node in the North East freelance network.
organisations.unresolved: Company identity, precise location and any organisational transfer to Audiogenic remain open.

organisations.name: Audiogenic / ASL
organisations.type: external publisher with Newcastle development activity
organisations.summary: External publisher/network associated with former Icon authors and BBC/Electron games; not treated as a North East-founded company.
organisations.role: Publishing, commissioning and possible rights/assets transfer; public record needs careful separation.
organisations.unresolved: Whether assets, staff, rights or only individual relationships transferred from Icon needs corroboration.

organisations.name: Superior Software
organisations.summary: Important Acorn-market publisher outside the region that published work by North East authors and Reflections.
organisations.role: National publisher connecting local authors to wider markets.
organisations.unresolved: Individual contracts and employment status should not be inferred from credits alone.

organisations.name: Micro Power
organisations.summary: Leeds publisher important to the Acorn market and to specific North East-authored work such as Gary Partis material.
organisations.unresolved: Contract terms and employment relationships remain unverified.

organisations.name: Reflections
organisations.summary: Newcastle development partnership/company associated with Martin Edmondson and Nicholas Chamberlain, early BBC/Electron work and later growth.
organisations.role: Original development and technical innovation, including early scrolling work.
organisations.unresolved: Early formation details should be corroborated with contemporary magazine material and official records.

organisations.name: Ubisoft Reflections
organisations.type: North East office/development studio of external owner
organisations.summary: Later corporate form of Reflections after acquisition history; useful as an epilogue bridge, not the centre of Phase 0.
organisations.role: Large-scale studio and Ubisoft development site.
organisations.unresolved: Detailed acquisition chain is outside Phase 0 and should remain concise.

organisations.name: Zeppelin Games
organisations.summary: North East company in the poster lineage; later name chain points toward Merit/Eutechnyx records.
organisations.role: Development and publishing after the first 8-bit boom.
organisations.unresolved: Brian Jobling's precise Tynesoft link and the founding narrative need better contemporary evidence.

organisations.name: Eutechnyx
organisations.summary: Later company name in the Zeppelin/Merit/Eutechnyx legal record; included only as a lineages-forward example.
organisations.role: Later legal/name lineage and skills bridge.
organisations.unresolved: Detailed corporate history is outside the Phase 0 core.

organisations.name: Flair Software
organisations.summary: Post-Tynesoft staff/founder heritage lead, not a legal continuation unless proven by records.
organisations.unresolved: Legal formation and ownership details require official corroboration.

organisations.name: I-Space Interactive
organisations.summary: Sparse record lead around titles such as Whizz and Australian Rugby League; retained as an open question.

organisations.name: Rage Newcastle

organisations.name: Venom Games
